water_temp_read.read: Water is warming; trout may favor more oxygenated water.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: The Chattahoochee River currently offers fair to good trout fishing with warming water favoring oxygenated runs. Recent stocking boosts confidence in using eggs and stocked-trout attractors.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning is best due to low wind and mild sky cover, which create stable conditions supporting early active nymph tactics in riffles and pocket water.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 62.8°F, flow is 899 cfs, gage height is 2.03 ft, and turbidity is 5.9 NTU at Norcross, GA.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages 20%, pressure is steady at 30.05 inHg with a neutral trend, winds average 5.8 mph from the west, and rain probability is low at 27% with minimal expected precipitation.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will seek comfortable feeding lanes near riffles, runs, and structure, favoring oxygenated water. Use clean drifts with smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and Duracell nymphs during early and late windows with a balanced nymph approach.
